name: Herbed Pork Loin
tagline: Roast pork loin with garlic, rosemary, thyme — pink and juicy

hero.alt: Sliced pork loin with herb crust on a wooden board
hero.caption: pork loin — herb crust, blush center

healthFacts: Pork loin is leaner than chicken thighs — ~26g protein, low fat per 3oz.
Cooking pork to 145°F (medium) is USDA-safe and yields a blush-pink, juicy slice.

steps.title: Make the herb paste
steps.detail: Mince garlic, rosemary, thyme. Stir into olive oil with Dijon, salt, pepper.
steps.tip: A mortar and pestle for the paste makes it cling — but a knife and bowl works fine.

steps.title: Slather the loin
steps.detail: Pat pork dry. Rub the paste all over, pushing into any folds. Rest 30 minutes at room temp — the seasoning settles and the meat doesn't shock-cook.
steps.tip: Don't skip the rest — cold-from-the-fridge meat tightens up in the oven.

media.alt: Pork loin rubbed with herb paste
media.caption: slather the loin

steps.title: Sear hard
steps.detail: Heat a skillet over high. Sear pork on all sides until browned, ~6 minutes total.
steps.tip: Crust = flavor. Don't move the loin until the side releases on its own.

steps.title: Finish in oven
steps.detail: Transfer to 375°F oven and roast 25–30 minutes until 145°F (63°C) at the thickest point. Tent and rest 10 minutes before slicing.
steps.tip: A tiny pink blush in the slice is correct — pork dryness comes from overcooking.
